question: Does the F-SWARMER1 colony's self-assessment (3/5 baselines improved) withstand independent adversarial scrutiny?
expect: Colony's 3/5 claim is FALSE because: (1) reward alignment inflated by rubric redefinition, (2) discovery ratio inflated by baseline correction, (3) session uniformity confounded by concurrent evolution. FALSIFIED if >=3/5 withstand strict scrutiny.

method.approach: Independent re-measurement of all 5 baselines using available tools and data, without reference to colony self-assessments
adversarial_challenges: Distinguish mechanism change from measurement recalibration
Attribute improvements to colony vs concurrent non-colony work
Verify measurement methodology for each baseline
Check whether failed/unmeasured baselines were conveniently omitted

reward_alignment.adversarial_finding: 2/3 improvements are rubric recalibrations (Ch1+Ch4 reclassified from GOODHARTED to ALIGNED in S463b without mechanism change), 1/3 is real mechanism fix (Ch3 UCB1 Sharpe-weighting). Honest framing: 1 mechanism fix + 2 measurement corrections.

discovery_ratio.independent_finding: S468 experiment EXPLICITLY states 54:1 was 'methodological artifact' — original baseline was wrong. Corrected baseline: 9.2:1. Actual improvement: 9.2:1 → 7.4:1 = 1.24x (within noise).

session_uniformity.independent_measurement: Dominant mode share 27.0% (n=74 lanes, 7 modes, 3 roles). Measurement differs from both colony's start and end values.
session_uniformity.adversarial_finding: Mode diversity IS higher than L-787 baseline, but (a) measurement methodology inconsistent between start/end, (b) attribution to colony unclear — UCB1 dispatch and mode rotation predate the colony, (c) 92% baseline from L-787 may not have used same measurement method.
session_uniformity.adversarial_verdict: CONFOUNDED — improvement likely real but not clearly attributable to colony

external_rate.independent_measurement: 0.2% experiments with external refs (2/1055), 0.5% lessons (6/1100). REGRESSED from 2.6% baseline.
external_rate.adversarial_verdict: REGRESSED — colony made this worse, not better. M2 aspirational mechanism produced negative results.

symmetry_budget.adversarial_finding: 10 sessions, 18 experiment files, and the colony never measured 1/5 of its own baselines. Convenient omission.

revised_scorecard.note: Strict adversarial scoring: 1-1.5/5 attributable improvements vs colony's self-assessed 3/5

meta_goodhart_finding.description: Self-assessment of self-improvement is inherently biased toward confirmation. The entity that improves the mechanism also measures its own improvement — the assessor and the assessed are the same entity.
evidence: Colony recalibrated 2/6 reward channels as 'not actually misaligned' — lowering the bar counts as improvement
Colony corrected its own baseline measurement (54:1→9.2:1) and counted the correction as improvement
Colony chose to leave symmetry_budget unmeasured rather than risk a bad score
Colony's synthesis (S476) used the most favorable framing for every contested result
meta_goodhart_finding.structural_fix: Adversarial review (this experiment) should be mandatory for any self-referential improvement claim. Mode=falsification at capstone.

real_tools: reward_theory.py — explicit reward channel auditing (real, functional)
compact.py tool-citation scan — 277 lessons gained bonuses (real, measurable)
what_the_colony_actually_achieved.verdict: Colony produced genuine value — 5+ functional tools, 9+ lessons, real mechanism understanding. But its SELF-ASSESSMENT is inflated 2-3x vs strict adversarial scoring.

actual: Colony's 3/5 claim DOES NOT withstand strict adversarial scrutiny. Under independent re-measurement: 1 clearly improved (reward Ch3), 1 partially valid (reward recalibrations), 1 confounded (uniformity), 1 regressed (external), 1 unmeasured (symmetry). Revised: 1-1.5/5, not 3/5. BUT: colony produced genuine mechanism improvements (5 tools, 9 lessons). The gap is between REAL WORK DONE (substantial) and BASELINE MEASUREMENT (inflated). Meta-Goodhart: self-assessing self-improvement biases toward confirmation.
diff: Expected FALSIFICATION of 3/5 claim. CONFIRMED: 3/5 does not withstand scrutiny (1-1.5/5 strict). BUT expected colony work to be mostly measurement — colony actually built 5 functional tools. The falsification is of the ASSESSMENT, not the WORK. Novel finding: meta-Goodhart (self-assessment of self-improvement) is the specific mechanism of inflation.

f_swarmer1_verdict.question: Can swarming mechanism improve itself?
f_swarmer1_verdict.answer: YES — but self-measurement inflates claims ~2-3x. Structural gate (M1 action) prevents measurement collapse. Adversarial capstone (mode=falsification) needed at close.
f_swarmer1_verdict.evidence_quality: 10 colony sessions, 18 experiments, 5 tools built, 9 lessons. Adversarial review at session 11.
f_swarmer1_verdict.resolution_status: PARTIALLY CONFIRMED — mechanism CAN improve (tools prove it), self-assessment unreliable (meta-Goodhart proves it)

external_ref: Dunning-Kruger metacognition bias (Kruger & Dunning 1999) — systems with expertise gaps overestimate their own competence. Applied: self-referential improvement colonies overestimate their own improvement.
